mapping.question: q1
mapping.objective_ref: Name and describe all four cells of a confusion matrix (TP, FP, FN, TN) in plain language.

mapping.question: q2
mapping.objective_ref: Construct a simple confusion matrix from a worked scenario and read off counts from it.

mapping.question: q3
mapping.objective_ref: Explain why a model can report 95% accuracy while still failing badly on the cases that matter most.

mapping.question: q4
mapping.objective_ref: Identify which cells of the confusion matrix feed the precision formula and which feed the recall formula.

mapping.question: q5
mapping.objective_ref: Describe at least one real-world situation where the confusion matrix reveals a problem that accuracy hides.

scope.note: No F1/ROC/AUC present. All acronyms (TP, FP, FN, TN) defined in corpus. All questions answerable from this topic alone.

verdict: Pass — all 5 checks clean. Cycle-2 distractor extension resolved all 3 prior length-bias failures (q3/q4/q5 ratios now 0.78/0.64/0.95); longest-correct rate dropped to 1/5 (0.20), below the 0.40 threshold. Positional bias at ceiling (0.40) but within limit. All distractors are plausible beginner misconceptions. All 5 LOs covered. No out-of-scope terms.
